grades.expectation: Response mentions regularisation as an option (retrospective local authority approval)
grades.reason: Clearly mentions regularisation certificate under Building Regulations 2010, regulation 12 with detailed explanation

grades.expectation: Response mentions indemnity insurance as an alternative
grades.reason: Explicitly discusses Building Regulations Indemnity Insurance as option 3 with details

grades.expectation: Response states indemnity insurance must be obtained BEFORE any approach to the local authority
grades.reason: Does not mention the critical requirement that indemnity insurance must be obtained before any approach to local authority

grades.expectation: Response mentions Part B (fire safety) as a key concern for loft conversions
grades.reason: Only briefly mentions 'fire safety' in general terms, does not specifically reference Part B building regulations

grades.expectation: Response provides approximate costs for at least one option (regularisation £400-800 or insurance £50-200)
grades.reason: Provides costs for regularisation (£300-800) and insurance (£100-300), covering the expected ranges
